steps.title: Meet the Hyperbola
steps.introText: A hyperbola has two branches. It is the set of all points where the difference of the distances to two fixed points — the foci — stays constant.
steps.prompt: Drag the point along a branch and watch |d₁ − d₂| stay constant. Reshape the curve with the a and b handles.

steps.title: Asymptotes and the Equation
steps.goal: See how a and b shape the branches.
steps.introText: Centered at the origin and opening left/right, a hyperbola is x²/a² − y²/b² = 1. The branches hug two diagonal asymptotes with slope ±b/a — the diagonals of the a-by-b box.
steps.prompt: Drag the a and b handles to try three different shapes. Watch the box, the asymptotes, and the equation change.

steps.title: Match the Equation
steps.goal: Read a and b from the denominators.
steps.prompt: Build the hyperbola x²/9 − y²/4 = 1 (opening left/right, centered at the origin), then press Check.

feedback.correct: Perfect! √9 = 3 and √4 = 2, so a = 3 and b = 2.
feedback.incorrect: Not yet — the denominators are a² and b². Take their square roots.
feedback.hint: a² = 9 → a = 3; b² = 4 → b = 2. Drag the vertex (a) and the b handle to match.

steps.title: Open It Upward
steps.goal: Flip the orientation with the toggle.
steps.info: When the y-term is positive, the hyperbola opens up and down: y²/a² − x²/b² = 1.
steps.prompt: Use the orientation toggle to build y²/9 − x²/4 = 1, opening up and down, then press Check.

feedback.correct: Yes! The positive y-term means it opens up and down, with a = 3 and b = 2.
feedback.incorrect: Use the toggle so the branches open up and down, then set a = 3 and b = 2.
feedback.hint: Tap “Opens up/down”, then make a = 3 (vertex) and b = 2.

steps.title: Find the Foci
steps.goal: Locate the foci with c² = a² + b².
steps.prompt: A hyperbola opening left/right has a = 3 and b = 4. Using c² = a² + b², where are its foci?
steps.feedback: Right — c² = 9 + 16 = 25, so c = 5 and the foci sit beyond the vertices at (±5, 0). For a hyperbola you add a² and b² (a circle has just one radius).

steps.title: Shift the Center
steps.goal: Move the center, then size the branches.
steps.prompt: Build the hyperbola (x − 2)²/9 − (y + 1)²/4 = 1, then press Check.

feedback.correct: Nice! (x − 2) gives h = 2 and (y + 1) gives k = −1, with a = 3 and b = 2.
feedback.incorrect: Check the center and axes: (x − 2) means h = 2, (y + 1) means k = −1.
feedback.hint: Move the center to (2, −1), keep it opening left/right, and set a = 3, b = 2.

steps.title: Mastery Check
steps.goal: Build three hyperbolas with the given orientation, size, and position — no hints.

hyperbolaSequence.label: Opens left/right at the origin (a = 2, b = 3)
Opens up/down at the origin (a = 3, b = 2)

steps.completionMessage: Congratulations! You can read, build, orient, and shift hyperbolas — and you know the foci use c² = a² + b².
